channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Calgary Councillor Calls for Measures to Improve Safety at Northeast Intersection After Child Hit
item.description: A Calgary city councillor is urging immediate action to improve safety at the intersection of Taradale Drive and Taravista Way in northeast Calgary after a 12-year-old boy, Bentley St. John, was struc...

item.title: MMDA inaugurates shower and changing facilities for cyclists in Pasig
item.description: The Metropolitan Manila Development Authority (MMDA) has officially opened shower facilities for bicycle commuters at the MMDA Motorcycle Riding Academy in Pasig City on March 17, 2026. The initiative...

item.title: CAF Awards Morocco AFCON 2025 Title After Senegal Forfeits Final
item.description: The Confederation of African Football (CAF) don overturn the 2025 Africa Cup of Nations (AFCON) final wey Senegal bin win, officially give Morocco the trophy after appeal. CAF talk say Senegal team vi...

item.title: Arsenal Secure Champions League Quarter-Final Spot with 2-0 Win Over Bayer Leverkusen
item.description: Arsenal FC don show say dem strong for Europe as dem beat Bayer Leverkusen well well for the second leg of their UEFA Champions League round of 16 tie. The match wey end 2-0 for Arsenal (3-1 on aggreg...

item.title: US Counterterrorism Director Resigns, Criticizes Iran War as Unjustified
item.description: One of the top US counterterrorism officials, Joe Kent, don resign from him job as Director of the National Counterterrorism Center because him no fit continue dey support the war wey dey go on for Ir...

item.title: Three Die While Cleaning Septic Tank at Raipur Hospital Due to Suspected Toxic Gas
item.description: On late Tuesday evening, three individuals tragically lost their lives while cleaning a septic tank at Ramkrishna Care Hospital in the Pachpedi Naka area of Raipur, Chhattisgarh. According to official...

item.title: OECD Report Calls for Stronger Competition Oversight in Kenya
item.description: Kenya has been advised to step up its enforcement of competition laws to enhance fair play in the economy and make markets more accessible, especially for small enterprises and consumers. According to...

item.title: Gemini 8: Neil Armstrong and Dave Scott Survive the First Major In-Orbit Emergency
item.description: The Gemini 8 mission, launched on March 16, 1966, marked a pivotal moment in NASA's space program, bridging the early Mercury missions and the later Apollo lunar expeditions. Piloted by first-time ast...

item.title: Shinyanga Airport and Kolandoto-Mhunze-Mangongo Road Projects to Improve Regional Transport and Trade
item.description: The Tanzanian government is pushin’ ahead with key infrastructure developments in Shinyanga to stimulate economic growth. Works Minister Abdallah Ulega has approved daytime aircraft landings at Shinya...

item.title: Japan Prime Minister to Discuss Missile Defense Collaboration with U.S. at Washington Summit
item.description: Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi is preparing to discuss Japan’s cooperation on the U.S. 'Golden Dome' next-generation missile defense system during her upcoming summit with President Donald Tru...

item.title: Roblox linked to extremist influence on children in the Philippines, authorities report
item.description: Authorities in the Philippines have raised concerns over the gaming platform Roblox being used as a tool for extremist indoctrination. Police in Calabarzon successfully foiled a planned mass shooting ...

item.title: Rising Oil Prices Amid Middle East Conflict Push Up Costs of Key Grocery Items in Canada
item.description: Canadians may see higher grocery bills in the coming months if oil prices remain elevated due to the ongoing conflict in the Middle East. The recent escalation involving the U.S., Israel, and Iran has...

item.title: Highlights from the 2026 WRC Safari Rally in Kenya
item.description: The 2026 WRC Safari Rally in Naivasha delivered another thrilling edition of Kenya’s iconic motorsport event. Japanese driver Takamoto Katsuta made history by clinching his first WRC victory, demonstr...

item.title: UK Government to Take Legal Action Against Roman Abramovich Over Frozen Chelsea Sale Funds
item.description: The UK government is moving toward legal action against Roman Abramovich, the former owner of Chelsea Football Club, after he failed to release £2.5 billion from the club's sale within a government-se...

item.title: New Cargo Ships at Karema Port Set to Boost Trade and Jobs on Lake Tanganyika
item.description: Construction of four big cargo ships at Karema Port, Katavi Region, has reached more than 95%, promising a boost for regional trade and local income. Two ships are fully completed, one is waiting for ...

item.title: Fatal House Fire on Trent Avenue Claims One Life
item.description: A tragic house fire occurred on Trent Avenue in East Kildonan on Monday evening, resulting in the death of one individual. Emergency crews responded to the fire at approximately 8:20 p.m., and firefig...

item.title: Trump Administration Allocates $540 Million to Repair and Improve California Water Infrastructure
item.description: The Trump administration has announced a $540 million investment in California’s water infrastructure to modernize and repair critical systems across the state. The largest portion, $235 million, will...

item.title: Northeast Monsoon Expected to Weaken Soon, Amihan Season to End
item.description: The Philippine Atmospheric, Geophysical and Astronomical Services Administration (PAGASA) announced that the northeast monsoon, locally known as amihan, is weakening and may end either tomorrow or by ...

item.title: MCD tells Delhi High Court it will not demolish Uttam Nagar homes without notice in Holi clash case
item.description: The Municipal Corporation of Delhi (MCD) has told the Delhi High Court that it will not carry out any demolition of homes in Uttam Nagar linked to the Holi‑day clash case without issuing a prior writt...

item.title: How Famous Nigerian Weddings Take Over Social Media From 2017 to 2026
item.description: For Naija, celebrity weddings no be small thing — dem dey always turn internet upside down. From the time of Banky W and Adesua Etomi for 2017 reach Simi and Femi Dapson for 2026, these love events do...
